channel.title: The Genome Brief
channel.description: A daily ~3-minute brief on viral clinical & wastewater genomics, biotech/pharma business news, AI in biomedicine, and a fun animal story to close.
channel.itunes:author: Nigel

item.title: Bundibugyo Ebola PHEIC update + Bedford lab on saltational SARS-CoV-2 + Jazz/AbCellera $4B TCE pact + BioBrain agentic microscopy + Cordyceps-mimic spider
item.description: WHO June 19 update on the Bundibugyo virus outbreak (DRC 896/232, Uganda 19/2). Bedford lab paper across 4.4M genomes traces SARS-CoV-2 saltational events to persistent human infections, not zoonosis. Jazz Pharmaceuticals and AbCellera ink an up-to-$4B T-cell engager pact in solid tumors. BioBrain, a multi-agent framework for natural-language microscopy analysis, quietly outperforms frontier LLMs on quantitative reproducibility. And Taczanowskia waska, a new Ecuadorian spider that mimics the Cordyceps-like fungus that normally kills its kind.
item.itunes:summary: Bundibugyo Ebola PHEIC update (WHO DON, 19 June 2026); Bedford lab paper finds SARS-CoV-2 saltational events trace to persistent human infections (bioRxiv, 19 June 2026); Jazz/AbCellera up-to-$4B T-cell engager collaboration (June 17, 2026); BioBrain multi-agent microscopy analysis framework (bioRxiv, 21 June 2026); Taczanowskia waska, an Amazonian spider mimicking the parasitic fungus that infects spiders (Zootaxa, June 2026).
